name: MDF Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for MDF. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for MDF surface cleaning without charring

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal MDF absorption and minimal substrate damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for efficient surface cleaning

fluenceThreshold.description: Energy density threshold for MDF surface ablation

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled material removal

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform surface treatment

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for thorough contamination removal

overlapRatio.description: Beam overlap percentage for complete surface coverage

dwellTime.description: Beam dwell time per spot for effective material removal

power.description: Typical range for industrial laser cleaning of wood-based materials like MDF to avoid charring; starting at 100W for light contamination. Max safe before substrate damage (thermal degradation) is ~500W at 1064 nm. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and studies on laser ablation of composites (e.g., J. Laser Appl. 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contamination removal on MDF; slower for heavy contamination (500-1000 mm/s) to ensure ablation, faster (2000-3000 mm/s) for light. Recommended 1500 mm/s balances efficiency and heat control. Source: Industry manuals from CleanLaser and process optimization in Opt. Lasers Eng. (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for MDF cleaning due to cost-effectiveness; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces HAZ but unnecessary for most industrial apps. 20 ns minimizes thermal effects on low-conductivity MDF. Source: Laser physics reviews in Appl. Phys. A (2019) on pulsed laser cleaning of organics.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for 1064 nm cleaning; 50 kHz recommended for MDF to achieve fast coverage without excessive heat accumulation, trading off speed vs. thermal buildup in resinous material. Higher rates (>80 kHz) risk damage. Source: Academic literature, e.g., J. Appl. Phys. (2021) on pulse repetition in ablation.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe removal of contaminants from MDF; damage threshold ~5-10 J/cm² due to charring/melting (MDF decomposition ~200-300°C). Recommended 2 J/cm² for effective cleaning below threshold. Source: Ablation studies in Wood Sci. Technol. (2017) and laser threshold measurements.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise industrial cleaning; 200 μm recommended for MDF to balance resolution on fibrous surface and large-area coverage, avoiding excessive heat concentration. Finer for details (<150 μm), larger for broad prep (300-500 μm). Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf lasers.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete MDF surface prep without overexposure. Source: Industry case studies in laser cleaning of wood composites (e.g., Proc. SPIE 2022).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage on irregular MDF surfaces; 60% recommended for optimal cleaning without gaps or over-ablation, typical range 30-80% based on scan patterns. Source: Process optimization in J. Mater. Process. Technol. (2019).
